meaning: adj.统治的,在位的,本届的,起支配作用的
examples: "The ancient Hawaiians were astronomers," wrote Queen Liliuokalani, Hawaii's last reigning monarch, in 1897.
The sky was dark, stars were twinkling high above, night was reigning, and everything was sunk in silken silence.
The reigning language of the day, C + +, was not suitable for this domain.
The Nash Conservatory was relocated from Buckingham Palace grounds in 1836 by reigning British monarch King William IV.
Reigning in inflation and providing affordable housing are important steps to foster a growing middle class.
